name: Captopril
executiveSummary: Short-acting ACE inhibitor; useful for sublingual hypertensive crisis and management of heart failure (HF) and hypertension (HTN) in the emergency setting.
indications:
## Indications

- Arterial hypertension and hypertensive crisis (sublingual according to protocol).
- Heart failure.
- Diabetic nephropathy.

## Precautions

- Contraindicated in pregnancy and patients with a history of angioedema.
- Monitor blood pressure, renal function, and potassium levels.

> Adjust per institutional protocol and medical prescription.

adulto.dose: Chronic HTN: 12.5–50 mg PO 2–3 times/day. Crisis: 25 mg sublingual, repeat according to protocol.

pediatrico.dose: 0.3–0.5 mg/kg/dose PO every 8–24 h (protocol maximums apply).

neonatal.dose: 0.01–0.05 mg/kg/dose PO every 8–24 h according to cardiologist.

stability:
## Stability

- Tablets according to package insert. Protect from moisture.
adverseEffects:
## Effects

- Hypotension, cough, hyperkalemia, rash, angioedema, taste disturbance.

bibliography.citation: American Heart Association. Hypertension and heart failure guidelines.
ANMAT. Información de medicamentos y prospectos autorizados en Argentina.
Sociedad Argentina de Cardiología. Guías de práctica clínica.
American Academy of Pediatrics. Medication guidance in critical care.
